text:
Market today: Chip stocks are getting hit hard - Samsung and SK Hynix down 9%+ signals a sector-wide rout spreading from Wall Street. This is the real driver today. Your kind of names (semis, leveraged chip ETFs) are in the crosshairs.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is leveraged 3x and sitting only ~$17 above your $200 stop - a further leg down in semis today could trigger it fast, and on margin that pain compounds. SPAL (-13.72%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF with almost no cushion above its $21.38 stop - this one could stop out today. SMH (-2.5%) is your cleaner semi exposure but still bleeding in this rout.

Watch: Whether SMH and SOXL break intraday lows - that tells you if the Samsung/Hynix selloff is infecting US semi names. Also watch if your SOXL stop at $200 gets tested.

Bottom line: Protect your leveraged positions first - margin turns a bad semi day into a portfolio wound.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line. With semis under pressure from the Samsung/Hynix rout today, be ready to act - don't let it blow through.
SPAL: Near-zero cushion above $21.38 stop. Brand-new leveraged ETF, moves violently - if it ticks there, honor the stop, no debate.
No new buys today - sector rout is active, no scanner setup has a real catalyst to override the tape.
